rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Friday, March 7, 2025, line 9. PETITIONER RAMONA MAYON'S Motion For Writ Of Mandate. The motion for writ of mandate is denied without prejudice. To obtain a writ of mandate, petitioner must serve the opposing party with the underlying petition, which does not yet appear to have occurred here, and must serve the opposing party with its motion for writ of mandate. Service of a motion must be made at least sixteen court days before the hearing, with additional time if the moving party uses a method of service other than personal service. Petitioner has not complied with these requirements here. The court cannot grant relief where notice has not been duly given to the party from whom relief is requested. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JEANINE OLSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO OPERA ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions; Declaration Of Avahit Ter-Mambreyan
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Friday, March 7, 2025, line 6. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO OPERA ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions. Defendant San Francisco Opera Association seeks terminating sanctions against Plaintiff Jeanine Olson. The Opera's papers establish that the Opera obtained an order compelling Olson to respond to discovery on January 14, 2025. Olson has not complied or responded. Olson has not opposed this motion. The Opera's motion to compel heard in January 2025 was preceded by a similar motion by cross-defendant Avatar Foods International to compel Olson to respond to discovery, which was granted on November 9, 2024. The court finds that Olson has engaged in a misuse of the discovery process. The court sanctions Olson as follows pursuant to CCP 2023.030(d)(2), (3): The matter is stayed in its entirety (including the cross-claim) until Olson complies with all outstanding court orders compelling discovery responses and compelling payment of sanctions. Olson may revive the action by moving to lift the stay and showing compliance. Because the passage of time may impede the parties' ability to litigate this case fairly, the court sets an outer bound on the stay and will issue a further terminating sanction if Olson has not complied with its orders. The court issues an Order to Show Cause re Dismissal to Olson. The OSC will be set for hearing on July 10, 2025, at 9 a.m. in Department 301. Olson must show she has fully complied with outstanding court orders compelling discovery responses and payment of sanctions by five court days before the hearing. If she fails to do so, the matter will be dismissed in its entirety, including the cross-claim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: Y.P. VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Friday, March 7, 2025, line 3. DEFENDANT SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY, LTD.'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's motion to strike is denied. The court excuses plaintiff's late filing of the First Amended Complaint. There is a liberal policy to hear cases on the merits and the late filing has not prejudiced defendant. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980 ["the law favors disposing of cases on their merits"].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
